response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has strong software engineering experience, particularly in performance optimization, system architecture, and hardware-software co-design across diverse domains. However, his profile lacks direct experience with AI accelerators, deep-learning workload optimization, SoC/IP modeling, and GPU architecture—core requirements for the Principal Performance Architect role. His background in embedded systems, real-time performance, and hardware development is relevant but insufficient for a specialized AI hardware role.
strengths: Extensive experience in performance optimization and low-latency system design (e.g., micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/sec, real-time metadata processing)
Proven track record in hardware-software co-design (e.g., robotic arm development with DLang/Java, NI LabWindows/CVI components)
Strong proficiency in multiple languages and paradigms, enabling adaptability to complex system design challenges
concerns: No documented experience with AI accelerators, deep-learning workload optimization, or GPU architecture—key requirements for the role
Lack of explicit SoC or IP modeling experience; resume highlights general software/hardware development rather than specialized performance architecture for AI chips
No advanced degree (PhD/Master's) in a relevant field; Bachelor's degree with 13+ years of experience may not meet the educational threshold without equivalent industry experience in AI hardware
